environment: real GitHub repo febufenn-cyber/cook-anything-staging-pub (dedicated staging; NEVER production) + local Supabase stack
design: commit-before-branch: quarantine blobs/tree/commit are created first, then the deterministic branch ref is created directly at the verified candidate commit. A crash between the two leaves only an orphaned, unreferenced commit (harmless); an empty deterministic branch can no longer exist, so any existing branch without this candidate's evidence file is refused as foreign.

scenarios.detail: crash left an orphaned commit and NO branch; the base branch then advanced; a resuming worker rebuilt against the new base, created the branch at the fresh verified commit, opened exactly one draft PR, recorded it

branch existed at the verified candidate commit; base advanced; resuming worker verified candidateId+contentHash on the branch, adopted it, opened exactly one draft PR against the advanced base, recorded it
PR existed unrecorded; resuming worker adopted it — no duplicate — and recorded pr_open
same-named branch with foreign content refused (existing_unrelated_branch_refused); nothing overwritten; no PR
non-allowlisted repository rejected before any side effect

scopeNote: Resolution covers GitHub-side crash resumption for the tested crash points including base-branch advancement between crash and resume. Full end-to-end candidate lifecycle on hosted Supabase remains pending the staging project; the operator stays disabled.
invariantsProven: candidate-ID + content-hash verification before branch reuse
atomic claim + single-use token (migration 000700) gates recording
one PR per candidate under crash+resume+base-advance
